mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to suppressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This mechanism operates continuously when the zero lower bound constraint is binding.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015-11-15 indicates sustained near-zero policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE falling > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months) signals tightening labor markets and rising wage pressures. This triggers equity market concerns about margin compression and inflation, producing negative forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth and discount rate expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% as of 2015-11-15 near cyclical lows
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite labor market tightening signal, or the correlation between 3-month UNRATE change and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below its 6-month moving average, signaling weakening consumer demand and economic momentum. This threshold crossing triggers a negative equity signal as investors reprrice earnings growth expectations downward over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth rate falls below 6-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between RSAFS growth deceleration and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits a month-over-month decline, signaling contraction in real economic activity and capacity utilization. This threshold crossing predicts negative SPX returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices near-term earnings and growth expectations in response to supply-side weakness.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 62% of instances following month-over-month INDPRO declines, or the correlation between INDPRO monthly change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) move in opposite directions over a 2-month window, signaling a demand composition shift away from goods consumption toward housing investment. This interaction indicates portfolio reallocation away from consumer discretionary equities, producing negative SPX returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 3% while RSAFS falls > 1% over 2-month window
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1060.0K and RSAFS at 448117.0M as of 2015-11-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite divergent HOUST/RSAFS signals, or the interaction term (HOUST rise × RSAFS fall) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K per month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains stable or rises, signaling labor market slack emergence. This interaction indicates the Fed will maintain accommodative policy longer than previously priced, producing positive SPX returns over the next 60 trading days as discount rates remain compressed.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100K and UNRATE rises > 0.1pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142900.0K and UNRATE at 5.0% as of 2015-11-15
regime_hint: labor market slack emergence with policy accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (low PAYEMS growth × rising UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 0.5%, creating a negative real rate regime. In this regime, equity valuations compress as investors demand higher nominal returns to compensate for inflation erosion, producing negative SPX returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.111 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2015-11-15
regime_hint: negative real rates with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of triggered instances despite negative real rate signal, or the interaction term (high inflation × low rates) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) remain elevated above 1000K units, signaling a demand composition shift toward non-productive housing investment relative to business capital formation. This regime indicates structural growth headwinds, producing negative SPX returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reprice long-term earnings potential.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16417.8B and HOUST at 1060.0K as of 2015-10-15
regime_hint: low-growth regime with elevated housing investment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2% and HOUST > 1000K, or the correlation between this regime indicator and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).
